## Why It Matters

This deeper routing page covers the concrete file conventions that matter most when reading or extending a Next.js docs app: `page` files, `layout` files, nested segments, dynamic routes, `searchParams`, and `Link` navigation.

## Key Excerpts

> "Next.js uses file-system based routing, meaning you can use folders and files to define routes."

> "A page is UI that is rendered on a specific route."

> "A layout is UI that is shared between multiple pages."

> "Wrapping a folder name in square brackets (e.g. `[slug]`) creates a dynamic route segment."

## Capture Notes

- The fetched content resolved to the current `Layouts and Pages` guide, which appears to be the modern replacement for the requested routing path.
- The core signals are the required root layout, nested layout composition, dynamic segments, and the distinction between server-side `searchParams` and client-side `useSearchParams`.
- The page also highlights `PageProps` and `LayoutProps` helper types generated by the Next.js type system.
